title: Multimodal RAG for Documents, Images, Tables, Layout, and Audio · Python Build Room
summary: A retrieval engineer and domain partner incrementally build accessible multimodal evidence retriever, change one query, document, tenant, language, model, index, or policy condition, and keep earlier Python, provenance, accessibility, security, evaluation, and evidence invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of accessible multimodal evidence retriever while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pr…
objectives: Preserve pages, regions, coordinates, reading order, figures, captions, tables, formulas, code, audio time spans, OCR confidence, and source links.
Compare OCR-text, image embedding, region embedding, and multi-vector late-interaction retrieval by modality and target evidence.
Retrieve tables and charts with title, units, headers, cells, footnotes, series, axes, source coordinates, and calculation grain.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Rag Multimodal Ingestion Layout
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop RAG engineering team handles case 1109 during an incident-response knowledge search. Build deterministic Python evidence to retrieve text, tables, images, layout, and audio evidence with coordinates and accessible alternatives; record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Preserve pages, regions, coordinates, reading order, figures, captions, tables, formulas, code, audio time spans, OCR confidence, and source links.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Rag Multimodal Ingestion Layout.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for preserve pages, regions, coordinates, reading order, figures, captions, tables, formulas, code, audio time spans, ocr confidence, and source links. For RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Rag Multimodal Ingestion Layout, keep Rag Multimodal Ingestion Layout connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

steps.title: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-multimodal-accessibility-eval-1s3wd8" that uses case 6603 to retrieve text, tables, images, layout, and audio evidence with coordinates and accessible alternatives. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval into observable performance evidence. For RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval, keep Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-multimodal-accessibility-eval-1s3wd8
# Competency: rag-multimodal-accessibility-eval.
# Case 6603: an enterprise retrieval migration canary.
# Operating constraint: preserve source provenance, tenant authorization, deletion, freshness, citation offsets, and untrusted-data separation.
# Changed case: remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer.
def evidence_rag_multimodal_accessibility_eval_1s3wd8(pages: int = 8, text_regions: int = 20, image_regions: int = 6, table_regions: int = 4, coordinates_kept: bool = True, text_alternatives: bool = True, modality_evaluated: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1s3wd8 = "1s3wd8-8dw3s1-6603"
    assert evidence_variant_1s3wd8
    evidence_axes_1s3wd8 = (
        "corpus1s3wd8",
        "query8dw3s1",
        "tenant6603",
        "index1s3wd86603",
        "prompt8dw3s16603",
        "evaluation66031s3wd8",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1s3wd8)) == 6
    if pages <= 0 or min(text_regions, image_regions, table_regions) < 0:
        return False, 0, "document-regions"
    if not coordinates_kept:
        return False, pages, "visual-citation"
    if not text_alternatives:
        return False, pages, "accessibility-alternative"
    if not modality_evaluated:
        return False, pages, "modality-quality"
    regions = text_regions + image_regions + table_regions
    assert regions >= pages
    return True, regions, "multimodal-evidence"
